install.title: Install MeerCOP
install.subtitle: Install the laptop anti-theft app
install.launchFromHome: Launch MeerCOP from your home screen.
install.iosStep1: Tap the Share button at the bottom of Safari
install.iosStep2: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
install.iosStep3: Tap "Add" in the top right corner
install.browserInstall: Select "Install App" or "Add to Home Screen" from the browser menu.

sections.appIntroContent:
MeerCOP is a real-time monitoring app designed to prevent laptop theft and unauthorized use.

When monitoring is enabled from your smartphone, any motion, touch, or lid opening detected on the laptop triggers an immediate alarm with photos, location, and live streaming.

📌 Key Features:
• Real-time alerts and notifications
• Live camera streaming from laptop
• GPS/IP-based location tracking
• Auto photo capture on alert
• Camouflage mode (screen appears off)
• Remote screen lock and messaging
• Multi-device management
• 17 language support
sections.createAccountDesc: Sign up at the MeerCOP website (meercop.com) with your email and password. Click the verification link sent to your email to complete registration. After verification, you can log in on the smartphone app with the same email/password.
sections.installLaptopDesc: Open a web browser (Chrome/Edge recommended) on your laptop and go to the MeerCOP site. Enter the serial number provided at purchase to register the device. Once registered, the laptop automatically goes online and syncs with your smartphone. Closing the browser will take the laptop offline.
sections.installPhoneDesc:
Open the MeerCOP site on your smartphone's web browser.

Android: An "Install App" popup appears automatically. If not, tap the browser menu (⋮) → "Install App" or "Add to Home Screen".

iOS (iPhone): Only Safari is supported. Tap the share button (□↑) at the bottom → "Add to Home Screen" → "Add".

⚠️ On iOS, the app must be installed as a PWA for push notifications to work.
sections.installPhonePermissionsDesc:
On first launch, permission request popups will appear:

• Notifications — Required for receiving push alerts when threats are detected
• Camera — Required for live streaming features
• Location — Required for location tracking

We strongly recommend setting all permissions to "Allow". You can change them later in browser settings.
sections.mainScreenOverview: The main screen consists of the following elements:
sections.headerAreaDesc: • ☰ Menu button (left) — Opens the side menu • Device Management button (right) — Goes to the device management screen • Alert bell icon — Shows unread alert count; tap to view alert history
sections.deviceSelectionBarDesc: Located below the header, it displays your registered laptops. With multiple devices registered, tap to switch between monitoring targets. The currently selected device is highlighted. Tap the bar to expand/collapse the list.
sections.statusIconsDesc: Four icons at the top center of the screen: • 📍 Location — View laptop's current location on map • 📷 Camera — Watch live video from laptop camera • 🌐 Network — Check laptop's network connection status • ⚙️ Settings — Change device-specific settings Icon colors indicate active/inactive status.
sections.characterAreaDesc: The MeerCOP character is displayed in the center. Below it, the current status message appears: • "MeerCOP is ready!" — Monitoring is off, standby mode • "MeerCOP is monitoring your laptop." — Monitoring is active • "🚨 Impact detected on your laptop!" — Alert triggered • "⚠️ Cannot connect to computer." — Laptop offline • "⚠️ Network connection lost." — Network disconnected
sections.monitoringToggleDesc: A large circular button at the bottom of the screen. • MeerCOP ON — Starts monitoring. All detection sensors on the laptop are activated. • MeerCOP OFF — Stops monitoring. The character image and background color change based on monitoring status.
sections.camouflageModeDesc: A monitor-shaped icon next to the monitoring toggle. Tapping this button: • Camouflage ON — The laptop screen is covered in black, appearing off. Monitoring continues. • Camouflage OFF — The laptop screen returns to normal. Useful for making the laptop appear unused when leaving your seat at a cafe, to detect theft attempts.
sections.monitoringOnDesc: When monitoring is enabled, the laptop's accelerometer, camera, keyboard/mouse input, and other sensors are activated. Any anomaly triggers an immediate alert to your smartphone and sounds an alarm on the laptop.
sections.motionDetectionDesc: Detected when the laptop is shaken or moved. Sensitivity adjustable: Sensitive (small vibrations), Normal, Insensitive (large movements only). 'Insensitive' recommended in vibration-prone environments like cafes.
sections.lidDetectionDesc: Detected when a closed laptop lid is opened. Only available on laptop devices; disabled on desktops/tablets.
sections.keyboardMouseDesc: Alerts trigger when unauthorized keyboard input or mouse movement is detected. Keyboard and mouse sensors can be toggled independently. Mouse sensitivity (sensitive/normal/insensitive) is configurable.
sections.cameraMotionDesc: Alerts trigger when movement is detected in front of the laptop camera. Camera permission must be granted. Sensitivity is adjustable.
sections.usbDeviceDesc: Alerts trigger when an unauthorized USB device is connected. Detects all USB connections including flash drives, external hard drives, keyboards, etc.
sections.powerChangeDesc: Alerts trigger when the charger is disconnected or connected. Useful for detecting attempts to unplug the charger and carry away the laptop.
sections.alertDesc:
When a sensor detects an anomaly, the following happen simultaneously:

• 📱 Push notification sent to smartphone
• 🔊 Alarm sounds on both smartphone and laptop
• 📷 Auto photo capture from laptop camera
• 📍 Current location auto-recorded
• 📺 Full-screen alert mode activated
sections.alertLiveStreaming: 🎥 Live Streaming — View real-time video from the laptop camera. Shows "Camera not detected" if no camera is available.
sections.alertLaptopLocation: 📍 Laptop Location — View the laptop's current location on a map. Location is displayed via GPS or IP-based tracking. Shows "No location info" if unavailable.
sections.alertCapturedPhotos: 📷 Captured Photos — Photos auto-captured by the laptop camera at the moment of detection. Photo download progress is shown, and after completion, photos can be viewed in grid/slide view.
sections.alertUnavailable: If a feature is unavailable, it shows "Not detected" or "No info" while the alert screen layout is maintained.
sections.alertDismissPhone: 🔕 Dismiss Phone Alarm — Stops only the smartphone alarm. The laptop alarm continues.
sections.alertDismissComputer: 🔇 Dismiss Computer Alarm (End Alert) — Remotely stops the laptop alarm as well. This ends the entire alert. If the laptop's PIN requirement is enabled, PIN entry is also required on the laptop.
sections.alertPhotoDetailDesc: Alert photos support the following features: • Grid View — View all photos as thumbnails at a glance • Slide View — View photos one by one in full size • Selection Mode — Select specific photos to save • Save All — Save all photos at once • Each photo includes change rate (%) and location info
sections.cameraFeatureDesc: Tap the camera (📷) icon on the main screen to enter the camera view. Live streaming starts automatically when entering the camera page.
sections.cameraLiveStreaming: 🎥 Live Streaming — Uses WebRTC technology for real-time video from the laptop camera. Mute/unmute toggle available.
sections.cameraSnapshot: 📸 Snapshot — Capture the current frame and save to your smartphone. Saved files go to your phone's download folder.
sections.cameraRecording: ⏺️ Recording — Record live video in WebM format. Recording duration is displayed, and the file is auto-saved when recording stops.
sections.cameraPause: ⏸️ Pause/Resume — Temporarily pause or resume the stream. The WebRTC connection is maintained.
sections.cameraSwitching: 🔄 Camera Switch — Switch between front/rear cameras if the laptop has multiple cameras.
sections.cameraAutoReconnect: 🔗 Auto Reconnect — If the camera connection is lost and restored, streaming automatically restarts.
sections.locationTrackingDesc: Tap the location (📍) icon on the main screen to view the laptop's current location on a map.
sections.locationGps: • GPS-based location — High accuracy (meters). Available on devices with GPS.
sections.locationAutoRecord: • Wi-Fi/IP-based location — For laptops without GPS, location is estimated using IP address. May have tens of meters to kilometers of error.
sections.locationAddress: • Address display — Coordinates are converted to actual addresses via reverse geocoding.
sections.locationHistoryDesc: View past location records in chronological order. Locations are automatically recorded when alerts trigger.
sections.locationMapFeaturesDesc:
• Zoomable interactive map
• Precise location marked with a pin
• Latitude/longitude coordinates displayed
• Last update time shown
• Refresh button to request latest location
sections.networkInfoDesc: Tap the network (🌐) icon on the main screen to view the laptop's network connection status.
sections.networkInfoDetailsDesc: • Connection Status — Online/Offline • IP Address — Currently assigned IP address • Connection Type — Wi-Fi, Ethernet, Cellular, Bluetooth, etc. • Speed — Network speed • Latency — Network latency (RTT) • Effective Grade — 2G/3G/4G grade 📡 Based on browser Network Information API. Actual speed may differ.
sections.settingsOverview: The Settings screen lets you change various settings per device. If multiple devices are registered, you can select the target device at the top.
sections.settingNicknameDesc: Change the display name of the laptop. This name is used in the device selector, alert history, etc. Duplicate names with other devices are not allowed.
sections.settingPinDesc: A 4-digit PIN required to dismiss alarms on the laptop. Default is '1234'. When 'PC alarm dismiss PIN' option is enabled, the laptop requires PIN entry to dismiss alarms.
sections.settingSoundDesc: Choose from 6 built-in alarm sounds (whistle, siren, bird, police siren, radio, quiet siren) or upload a custom audio file (under 5MB). Use the preview (▶) button to listen before selecting.
sections.settingVolumeDesc: Adjust alarm volume using the slider in the alarm sound settings popup. Applied to both laptop and smartphone.
sections.settingPhoneAlarmDesc: Toggle whether the smartphone plays alarm sound when an alert occurs. When off, alarm sound won't play but push notifications will still be received.
sections.settingPcPinDesc: When enabled, PIN entry is required to dismiss alarms on the laptop. When disabled, alarms can be dismissed without PIN on the laptop.
sections.settingSensorDesc: Enable/disable each detection sensor individually: • Camera Motion Detection — Detect movement in front of camera + sensitivity setting • Lid Detection — Laptop only, detect lid open/close • Keyboard Detection — Detect keyboard input • Mouse Detection — Detect mouse movement + sensitivity setting • USB Detection — Detect USB device connections • Power Detection — Detect power cable connect/disconnect
sections.settingSensitivityDesc: Camera motion and mouse detection have 3 sensitivity levels: • Sensitive — Reacts to small movements/vibrations • Normal — Reacts to typical movements • Insensitive — Detects only large movements (recommended for vibration-prone environments like cafes)
sections.settingSerialDesc: View registered serial numbers. Each serial shows its plan type (Trial/Basic/Premium) and connected device name. Tap a serial to copy to clipboard.
sections.settingStreamingQualityDesc: Set camera streaming resolution. Higher quality means better video but more data usage. Changes apply on the next camera connection.
sections.settingLanguageDesc: Change the app display language and laptop program language. Supports 17 languages and can be set independently per device. Smartphone app language can also be changed from the side menu.
sections.settingDeviceTypeDesc: Available sensors vary by device type (Laptop/Desktop/Tablet). Example: Lid detection is only available on laptops.
sections.deviceManageDesc: Tap the Device Management button on the right side of the header to manage all registered devices.
sections.deviceManageAdd: • Device List — View serial numbers, device names, plans, remaining days, and online/offline status at a glance
sections.deviceManageHistory: • Alert History — View alert history for each device
sections.deviceManageStatus: • Status Icons — Tap location, camera, network, or settings icons on each device card to directly access that device's features
sections.deviceManageSortDesc: • Name — Sort by device name alphabetically • Number — Sort by user-assigned number • Plan — Sort by plan: Premium > Basic > Trial • Days Left — Sort by expiration date (soonest first) • Monitoring — Show currently monitored devices first
sections.deviceManageBulkDesc: • Select All/Deselect — Select or deselect all devices at once • Monitor ON/OFF — Start/stop monitoring for multiple selected devices at once
sections.deviceManageDragDesc: Long-press the ≡ handle on the left of a device card and drag up/down to reorder. Changes are saved automatically.
sections.deviceManageNumberDesc: Assign a custom number to each device. Used for number-based sorting.
sections.sideMenuDesc: Tap the ☰ menu button at the top left of the main screen to open the side menu.
sections.sideMenuContentsDesc: • User Info — Displays the logged-in user's name and email • My Serials — Lists registered serial numbers with plan info and connected devices. Pagination is supported for 5+ serials • Edit Profile — Link to edit your profile on meercop.com • Q&A / Help — Opens this help page • Language Selection — Choose from 17 languages for the app interface • Logout — Safely log out
sections.remoteCommandsDesc: Remote command features are accessible from the device management screen.
sections.remoteCommandsLockDesc: Remotely lock the laptop screen. A lock screen is displayed, and the user must contact the administrator to unlock.
sections.remoteCommandsMessageDesc: Display a message on the laptop screen. Up to 500 characters. Example: Display a warning message like "This laptop is being monitored".
sections.remoteCommandsOffline: Remote commands cannot be sent when the device is offline.
sections.photoHistoryDesc:
View photos auto-captured during alerts in chronological order.

• Each alert entry shows the number of photos, time, and location info
• Tap a photo for full-screen view and saving
• Individual entries can be deleted
• Grid/slide view toggle available
sections.alertHistoryDesc: Tap the alert bell icon in the header to view all alert history. • All — Shows all alert history • 📸 Photo — Filters only alerts with photos • 🔔 Alert — Filters only general alerts In selection mode, you can select multiple alerts for bulk deletion. The "Mark All Read" button marks all alerts as read.
sections.pushNotificationDesc:
Push notifications are auto-subscribed when you select a device.

• Receive alerts even when the app is in the background
• Browser notification permission must be set to "Allow"
• On iOS, the app must be installed as a PWA for push support
• Notifications may be delayed in power saving or low power mode
sections.planInfoDesc: MeerCOP offers 3 plans: • 🌟 1-Day Trial (Free) — Try basic features • ⭐ Basic — Access core monitoring features • 👑 Premium — Unlimited access to all features Available features vary by plan. Attempting to use a locked feature will show an upgrade prompt. Plan renewal is available at meercop.com.
sections.licenseExpiredDesc: When your plan expires, an expiration overlay appears on the app screen. Renew your subscription at meercop.com and restart the app to reactivate.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
faq.q1: Does monitoring drain laptop battery significantly?
faq.a1: Sensor-based detection uses slightly more battery than normal, but very little when the screen is off. Disabling camera monitoring can further reduce battery consumption. Using camouflage mode helps save battery as the screen appears off.
faq.a2:
The laptop's local alarm works without internet. However, the following remote features require network connection:

• Smartphone notifications
• Photo transfer
• Live streaming
• Location tracking
• Remote alarm dismiss

A warning message appears on the main screen when disconnected, and auto-reconnects when the network is restored.
faq.a3:
Yes, register additional serial numbers to manage multiple laptops with one account.

• Switch between devices using the device selector at the top
• Each device can be monitored independently
• Bulk monitoring ON/OFF is available in device management
• Independent sensor settings per device
faq.a4: Two ways to resolve: 1. Change PIN: Go to smartphone app Settings → Alarm PIN and enter a new 4-digit number. 2. Remote Dismiss: Use the "Dismiss Computer Alarm" button on your smartphone to remotely dismiss without PIN. The default PIN is '1234'.
faq.q5: What happens when the laptop enters sleep mode?
faq.a5: In sleep mode, the browser and network are disabled, so monitoring won't work. The smartphone app shows "⚠️ Cannot connect to computer". 💡 Recommended settings: • Windows: Settings → System → Power → Set lid close action to "Do nothing" • Mac: System Settings → Battery → Disable auto-sleep • We recommend keeping the laptop plugged in
faq.q6: How do I change the alarm sound?
faq.a6: Tap "Alarm Sound" in Settings to open the settings popup. Built-in sounds (6 types): • Whistle, Siren, Bird, Police Siren, Radio, Quiet Siren Use the ▶ button next to each sound to preview. Custom alarm sound: Tap "Choose from device" to upload an audio file under 5MB. You can also adjust volume using the slider.
faq.q7: It says camera is not detected.
faq.a7: Check the following: 1. Webcam check — Verify the laptop has a webcam. For external webcams, check the USB connection. 2. Browser permission — Click the lock (🔒) icon in the laptop browser's address bar → Site settings → Set camera to "Allow". 3. Other apps — Close Zoom, Teams, or other apps using the camera, then try again. 4. Laptop app status — Make sure the MeerCOP browser tab is open on the laptop.
faq.a8: Most laptops lack GPS, so IP-based location tracking is used. • IP-based — May have km-level error. Based on ISP's IP allocation location. • Wi-Fi-based — Tens to hundreds of meters error. More accurate using Wi-Fi AP info. ⚠️ Wi-Fi/IP-based location may differ from actual location. Use as reference only. 💡 When using a VPN, the VPN server's location may be shown.
faq.q9: Where do I get a serial number?
faq.a9:
Serial numbers can be purchased at meercop.com.

• Select a plan and complete payment to receive a serial number
• The issued serial is also sent via email
• View and tap to copy registered serials in the settings page
• One serial number can only be linked to one laptop
faq.q10: Can I adjust alert sensitivity while monitoring?
faq.a10: Yes, you can change sensitivity anytime in settings. Changes are applied to the laptop immediately. Motion sensitivity (camera motion): • Sensitive — Reacts to small vibrations • Normal — Reacts to typical movements • Insensitive — Detects only large movements Mouse sensitivity: • Sensitive — Reacts to small mouse movements • Normal — Reacts to typical mouse use • Insensitive — Detects only large mouse movements 💡 'Insensitive' is recommended for vibration-prone environments like cafes.
faq.a11: Camouflage mode covers the laptop screen in black, making it appear off. How it works: • Covers the entire screen with a black overlay • The browser continues all monitoring in the background • Camera capture, location tracking, sensor detection all work normally Use cases: • When leaving your seat at a cafe • In libraries, offices, and public spaces • Makes the laptop appear unused to encourage and detect theft attempts
faq.q12: The app doesn't send push notifications.
faq.a12: Check the following in order: 1. Browser notification permission — Verify notifications are set to "Allow" in browser settings. 2. iOS PWA installation — On iOS Safari, push notifications only work when installed as a PWA. Opening directly in Safari won't enable push. 3. Device selection — A monitoring target device must be selected in the app for auto push subscription. 4. Power saving mode — Smartphone power saving or low power mode may delay or block notifications. 5. Restart app — If none of the above works, fully close and reopen the app.
faq.q13: What happens if I close the MeerCOP browser on the laptop?
faq.a13:
Closing the MeerCOP browser tab or window on the laptop will:

• Stop monitoring
• Switch the laptop to offline status
• Show "⚠️ Cannot connect to computer" on the smartphone app

💡 To maintain monitoring, keep the MeerCOP tab open and close the laptop lid (sleep must be disabled).
faq.q14: The app isn't working or shows errors.
faq.a14: Try these solutions: 1. Refresh browser — Refresh the app (for PWA, fully close and reopen). 2. Clear cache — Clear browser cache and log in again. 3. Internet connection — Check Wi-Fi or mobile data connection. 4. Update browser — Use the latest version of Chrome, Edge, or Safari. 5. Contact us — If issues persist, contact support@meercop.com.
faq.q15: Can I change the streaming quality?
faq.a15: Yes, go to Settings → Streaming Quality to change it. Higher quality means clearer video but more data usage. Changes apply on the next camera connection. High quality recommended on Wi-Fi, lower quality on mobile data.
faq.q16: Can I monitor from multiple smartphones simultaneously?
faq.a16: You can log in from multiple smartphones with the same account. However, camera streaming is only available on one smartphone at a time. Monitoring ON/OFF and settings changes can be made from any device, and changes sync in real-time.
faq.q17: How much data does it use?
faq.a17: Approximate data usage by feature: • Monitoring standby — Very little (heartbeat signals only) • Alert photo transfer — About 50KB-200KB per photo • Live streaming — About 3-10MB per minute depending on quality • Location tracking — Negligible 💡 Using Wi-Fi saves mobile data.
faq.q18: What happens if I delete a device?
faq.a18:
Deleting a device from device management:

• All alert history for that device is deleted
• The serial number is retained and can be linked to a new device
• Logging back into MeerCOP on the laptop registers it as a new device

permissions.title: Permissions Required
permissions.subtitle: MeerCOP needs the following permissions to provide full security features.
permissions.notificationDesc: Receive alert sounds and push notifications when threats are detected.
permissions.cameraDesc: View real-time camera feed from your monitored device.
permissions.locationDesc: Track the location of your monitored device.
permissions.deniedHelp: Go to browser settings to re-enable this permission.
